expect: Genesis seeds at N=1171: theoretical 3-hop reachability ~26%, practical hop-1 ~5%. Seed quality: top-3 should cover >=3 domains with --diverse flag. If practical:theoretical ratio >0.3 at hop-1, seeds convey enough context without chain-following.
actual: 10 seeds (L-601,L-912,L-526,L-599,L-895,L-950,L-813,L-787,L-1118,L-908) at N=1171. Forward 3-hop: 20.9% (245 lessons, down from 26.2% at N=1158). Reverse 3-hop: 56.1% (657). Bidirectional: 67.2% (787). Practical (hop-0+hop-1): 3.8% (45 lessons, down from 4.8%). Practical:theoretical ratio: 0.184 (BELOW 0.3 threshold). Gap: 5.4x. Seed domains: only meta + nk-complexity (2, not 3+). Domain coverage from citations: 14 domains, heavily meta-skewed (37/53 = 70%).
diff: Expected ~26% theoretical, got 20.9% — 5.1pp decline in 13 lessons. Expected ~5% practical, got 3.8% — declining faster than theoretical. Practical:theoretical ratio 0.184 < 0.3 threshold = BELOW. Seeds do NOT convey enough context without chain-following enforcement. Reachability is DECLINING with N — the attention-dilution effect. New lessons being added outside seed-reachable subgraph. Seed domains (2) below target (3+) even without --diverse flag — genesis_seeds.py --diverse only returned 3 seeds total (truncated output bug?).

comparison_to_L1268.trend: DECLINING — reachability eroding as corpus grows

conclusion: Chain-following enforcement is necessary. Seeds alone cannot bootstrap effective knowledge access at current scale. Additionally, reachability is declining — the seed set needs refreshing as N grows. Two structural fixes needed: (1) chain-following instruction in CLAUDE.md, (2) seed refresh protocol that re-selects seeds when reachability drops below threshold.
